response.justification: The candidate has genuine geospatial experience (GISCollective, Green Map GIS apps) and low-latency systems work in DLang at Dunnhumby, which demonstrates comfort with performance-oriented server development. However, modern C++ experience is thin and dated (a brief 2014–2015 stint at National Instruments), there is no Rust, and no experience building navigation guidance or routing pipelines specifically. Recent work has centered on TypeScript/Node full-stack product development, which is far from this role's core of C++ routing tile pipelines at scale.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial data platform experience (GISCollective, Green Map)
Low-latency, high-throughput systems work with async I/O and caching (DLang at Dunnhumby)
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, testing tools authorship
concerns: Modern C++ experience is minimal and roughly a decade old; no Rust
No routing, navigation guidance, or map-tile pipeline experience
Recent roles are TypeScript/Node web product work, not systems-level data pipelines
